title: Authentication, Sessions, Passwords, Bearer Tokens, and Credential Recovery · Program Defense
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for authentication, sessions, passwords, bearer tokens, and credential recovery and requires the smallest safe authentication and credential lifecycle policy with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for authentication and credential lifecycle policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate presented credential, verification, authenticated principal, session, assurance, freshness, and downstream authorization.
Use reviewed password hashing, bounded comparison, generic failures, rate and abuse controls, MFA or step-up policy, and secure recovery lifecycle.
Issue opaque sessions with rotation, server-side state or revocation, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Path, Domain, expiry, fixation defense, and logout invalidation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
